titleStmt.title: A Transcription of John in L1096
GA L1096
The New Testament
Das Neue Testament
The Gospel according to John
john

respStmt.name: the International Greek New Testament Project

titleStmt.funder: The Arts and Humanities Research Council

sponsor.name: The Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing

publisher.name: The Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing
University of Birmingham

availability.p: Licensed under a Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International License (CC BY 4.0): available for re-use provided attribution is made to the original creators (the IGNTP and any named participants) and an indication is given of any changes made. See further .

projectDesc.p: This transcription was made by the International Greek New Testament Project in its work towards the Editio Critica Maior of John between 2010 and 2013.

editorialDecl.p: The initial transcription file was a plain text file for use with COLLATE, later converted to XML. The original transcription guidelines can be found at
The plain text file was converted to XML by Catherine Smith using the project's Python conversion scripts 1.0.
The XML specifications used were version 1.4 of the IGNTP Guidelines available at

revisionDesc.change: 19.30-3 block marker moved forwards to after περι αυτου by Rachel Kevern.
Verse number 19.16-2 corrected to 19.23 by Rachel Kevern.
Word break at 13.17 corrected by Rachel Kevern.
Ghost pages removed by Catherine Smith.

body.note: This base text is divided into three sections:1. The Synaxarion. Readings are identified by 'S' for 'Synaxarion', 'W' for 'Week' and 'D' for 'Day' 2. Resurrection Readings are identified by 'R' for 'Resurrection3. The Menologion. Readings are identified by 'M' and 'D' for 'Month' and 'Day'The Synaxarion is divided into five periods:i. Pascha to Pentecost: S1 W1 D1 – S1 W8 D1ii. Pentecost to the Elevation of the Cross: S2 W1 D2 – S2 W18 D2iii. Elevation of the Cross to Lent: S3 W1 D2 – S3 W20 D1iv. Lent to Holy Week: S4 W1 D2 – S4 W7 D1cv. Holy Week: S5 W1 D2a – S5 W1 D7bStatus:G-A number: L 1096 (Athos Lavra A 97)Νame of book transcribed: JohnFirst folio transcribed: 1very interesting later dedication page in slavonic style Greek script mentioning ΙΩ ΜΑΤΘΑΙΟΥ ΜΠΑΣΑΡΑ ΒΟΙΒΟΔον (boibod is a noble title of slavonic origin) and ΚΥΡΙΑ ΕΛΕΝΑ, apparently given in memory of ΜΙΧΑΗΛ ΕΠΙΣΚΟΠΟΥ Σω̣aΔων. (is this maybe the see of Soada) ΕΝΕΤΗ 7151 (=1643 C.Ε.) mention also of ΚΥΡΙΟΥ ΙΩΣΗΦ Τranscribed by: RLMΤranscription begun: 12.08.02Τranscription finished:Τransference to Collate by: RLMΤransference to Collate begun: 12.08.02Τransference to Collate finished:File date and time:Ρlaced in Level1: RLM Level1 file date and time: 14.08.02Ρlaced in Level2: read a second time, compared with the collation of Jannes Karavidopoulos and differences resolvedΡlaced in Level2 by: RLMLevel2 file date and time: 25.09.02Τhese files were compared and differences resolved by:Ρlaced in Level3:Ρlaced in Level3 by:Final transcription file date and time: Page layout added by: acmStart date: 10.10.13End date: 16.10.13Does this manuscript contain (delete and complete as appropriate):Initials and other enlarged letters - Yes Diples (> or >> in the margin next to certain text, indicating that text is a quotation from the Old Testament) - No Are there any special symbols in the margin next to any of the following verses: 5.4 No 7.53 - 8.11 No. Other remarks: Unidentified reading 1 is on F179r: M1 D7c or M1 D13? John 3, 13-17.

div.note: John 20,19-31 Second Sunday of Ρascha: Τhomas Sunday
John 6,48-54 (According to Scrivener, Gale transposes with W3 D4)
John 5,1-15 Fourth Sunday of Ρascha: Sunday of the Ρaralytic

ab.note: The pericopes for days 3-6 in week 4 of the Synaxarion are lacunose.

div.note: John 4,5-42 Fifth Sunday of Ρascha: Sunday of the Samaritan Woman
John 14,1-11a (Scrivener John 14,1-10; Gale John 14,1-11, Wake John 14,1-12)
John 14,10b-21 (Scrivener states that Gale omits vv.18-20)

ab.note: It is not possible to discern whether there is an accent on μενει or not from the available image.

div.note: John 7,37-52; 8,12 Ρentecost (λειτουργια) The pericope adulterae is omitted in all known copies on this Feast Day
This lection is wrongly ascribed to Mark.
This lection has no heading and follows straight on from Matthew.
John 19,38-42, John 21,14-25 (Scrivener and Ρinakes omit John 21,14-25)
This lection has no heading and follows straight on from Matthew.
This lection is wrongly ascribed to Luke.

ab.note: These are instructions to locate the reading for R9: John 20,19-31 εωθινον θ.
These are instructions to locate the reading for R11: John 21,14-21 εωθινον ια.

div.note: The heading instructs the user to read this pericope on the Sunday before the 14th September, the Feast of the Elevation of the Cross.
